data.body: PRIOR-ART RATIONALE for the auto-init consent gate (user-requested record). Git-attached trackers fall in two camps: (1) silent implicit creation via ref-only storage — git-bug (refs/bugs/*, first 'git bug add' auto-creates), git-appraise (refs/notes) — no init, invisible footprint; (2) explicit init required — git-issue/dspinellis ('git issue init' creates .issues/), bugs-everywhere/sit ('be init'/'sit init'), Fossil (own SQLite, 'fossil new'). rebar sits in camp 2 and is MORE conservative than git-bug: explicit 'rebar init'/init_repo() OR interactive-confirmed auto-init ([Y/n] default Yes), NEVER silent in non-interactive (CI/pipe/library/MCP -> error). Justification: rebar's init mutates the WORKING TREE (orphan 'tickets' branch + linked worktree at .tickets-tracker/ + .git/info/exclude edits + .env-id/.closure-key), a heavier/visible footprint than git-bug's invisible refs — so silent materialization on a stray read would surprise; requiring consent matches the weight of the operation. Stricter-than-most point: rebar ERRORS on non-interactive auto-init rather than defaulting to create (git-bug would create; git-issue errors with no auto-init at all). Source: docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md (benchmarks git-bug/git-issue/Fossil/dstask) + maintainer knowledge (git-bug/git-issue/Fossil high-confidence; dstask/ticgit specifics unverified).
